input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the `BindingContext`. The XAML has bindings but the page doesn't know *which* object to bind to.

**Fix — set the BindingContext**, either in XAML:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:YourApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="YourApp.MainPage">
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

or in the code-behind constructor:

```csharp
public MainPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when properties change.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank because the `BindingContext` is never set. This is a straightforward knowledge question — no tools needed.
